chief_complaint: A 24-year-old gravida 2 para 1 woman presented with acute abdominal pain in her 24-week period of gestation.
history: Acute pancreatitis is an uncommon complication in pregnancy. The exact etiology in some cases cannot be identified and hence they are referred as idiopathic pancreatitis. The patient was managed accordingly.
exam: Vital signs and physical examination findings not provided.
labs: Laboratory reports suggested elevated serum amylase or lipase and other enzymes consistent with acute pancreatitis.
